courses.title: Professional Communication Skills
outcomes: Acknowledge multicultural communication differences in the nurse-client relationship.
Use active listening skills including restating, reflecting, clarifying, summarizing and empathy.
Use effective, culturally sensitive verbal and nonverbal communication with emotional intelligence.
Apply assertiveness, therapeutic communication and conflict-management principles.
Use structured clinical communication tools including SBAR, PQRSTU, PACT and DAIM.
Share information respectfully with clients and families and encourage participation in decisions.

courses.title: Clinical Pharmacology and Drug Administration I
outcomes: Explain core pharmacology terminology, history and principles.
Identify the purposes and sources of medications.
Differentiate major drugs and drug classes used in clinical practice.
Differentiate pharmacokinetics and pharmacodynamics.
Explain drug standards and legislation.
Explain indications, contraindications, effects and adverse effects of drugs affecting body systems.
Design and modify appropriate dosing regimens for specific patient populations within the learner scope.

courses.title: Medical Surgical Nursing I
outcomes: Apply integrated basic, clinical and nursing sciences when caring for adults with medical-surgical conditions.
Use clinical reasoning and the nursing process to solve patient problems and plan holistic care.
Use evidence and integrated concept maps in nursing care planning.
Plan care for hematological, immunological, fluid-electrolyte, acid-base, gastrointestinal, genitourinary and oncological disorders.
Demonstrate legal, ethical, culturally sensitive and collaborative nursing practice.
Formulate nursing care plans and provide safe client and family education.

courses.title: Health Assessment I
outcomes: Obtain a complete health history using appropriate interviewing and therapeutic communication skills.
Use appropriate assessment and physical-examination techniques for specified body regions.
Differentiate normal from abnormal health findings.
Document patient findings appropriately.
Integrate growth and development, anatomy, physiology and psychosocial knowledge when analysing assessment data.

courses.title: Pathophysiology I
outcomes: Explain homeostasis, cellular change, inflammation, infection, healing and abnormal immune responses.
Relate anatomy and physiology to disruptions in homeostasis and disease processes.
Differentiate disease processes associated with homeostatic and hematological disorders.
Interpret fluid, electrolyte and acid-base values and their role in disease.
Apply pathophysiology principles when caring for patients in hospital and community settings.
Explain common genitourinary and gastrointestinal disease mechanisms and clinical manifestations.
Explain environmental contributions to altered physiological processes.
